reason: Backend pre-development checklist and package entry point for API/WebSocket work.
Room session token, product-member authorization, and token logging rules apply to WebSocket handshake authentication.
Snapshot and join/leave broadcasts depend on SQLite room/member lifecycle and active-member semantics.
Router/startup wiring and module dependency changes must follow backend structure and config conventions.
Public error envelopes and stable error mapping are central to WebSocket pre-upgrade rejection behavior.
WebSocket token query parameters and credentials must not be logged.
WebSocket message envelopes cross HTTP/service/store/client contract boundaries.
Implementation should reuse token, authorization, and payload projection logic rather than duplicate contracts.
Curated evidence and anchors for Issue #15 scope and existing implementation seams.
